title: The Bandit Road
setting: The trade road north of Greyhollow has gone silent. Two merchants haven't returned. Smoke from a campfire was seen in the trees. The villagers ask you to walk the road — they need to know what waits there.

introVariations.text:
The road darkens as you ride past the third milestone. You smell woodsmoke before you see them.

Three figures step out from the brush — leather armor, swords drawn, eyes hard from a winter spent hungry. The leader is taller, his cloak red beneath the dust. He levels his blade at you with the easy confidence of a man who has done this before.

"Coin or blood, traveler. Your choice."

fight.successText:
Garric falls hard, his cloak catching the dust as he goes down. His minion drops his sword and runs into the trees, calling something behind him you don't quite catch.

You stand alone on the road. A merchant's purse, dropped in haste, gleams at the foot of an oak.
fight.failureText:
You wake in a ditch with the cold dawn on your face. Your purse is lighter, your pride lighter still. The bandits are gone, but you can hear their laughter on the wind for hours afterward.

On the way home, a farmer offers you bread and asks no questions.

charm.successText:
You speak slowly, as if to a frightened horse. You ask Garric where he learned to use that blade — really learned it. You mention the cold, the empty pantry, the children somewhere who must wonder where their father went.

His sword wavers. Lowers. He grunts something at his men, and they file back into the trees without a word. As they pass, Garric pauses.

"You're not what I expected, traveler. Don't make me regret this."
charm.failureText:
Garric laughs without humor. "Pretty words. Pretty enough to bury a man."

His blade comes up too fast for argument. His men close in around you, eyes lit with the kind of insult only a failed bargain produces.

flee.successText:
You spin your horse and ride hard, lashes of the trees stinging your face. Their shouts fade behind you. You're alive. You're whole. The road is theirs for now.

Greyhollow's elder shakes his head when you return. He says nothing aloud, but something passes across his face.
flee.failureText:
Your horse rears as Garric's minion grabs the reins. A blade flashes across your shoulder before you can wheel free.

"Cowards bleed first," Garric says.
